food_subgroup: Grilled minced meat wrap / flatbread roll

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for chicken kofta wrap; closest parent 'wrap sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002764) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat-based sandwich / wrap (chicken, grilled, with flatbread)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Turkish kofta wrap; A0FKP (meat-based sandwich/wrap) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for chicken kofta wrap or sufficiently close parent food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One chicken kofta wrap weighing approximately 280 g, consisting of grilled spiced minced chicken kofta, flatbread, fresh vegetables, and sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Kofta Wrap — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Ground chicken (cooked, grilled), Lavash flatbread, tomato, lettuce, and garlic yogurt sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for chicken kofta wrap as a composite dish. Values estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 45% grilled minced chicken (USDA FDC #171477 ground chicken cooked), 30% lavash/flatbread (USDA FDC #175023), 10% tomato and lettuce, 15% garlic yogurt sauce (USDA FDC #170893 plain yogurt + garlic). Per-100g values derived from weighted average of components. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of flatbread-based wraps with mixed protein and vegetable filling — analogous estimate based on pita bread GI (57, Atkinson et al. 2008) adjusted downward for high protein content and mixed filling composition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for chicken kofta wrap. Analogous estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database pita bread GI of 57 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). GI adjusted to approximately 47 to account for: (1) substantial protein content from grilled chicken kofta (~13.5g/100g) which attenuates postprandial glucose response; (2) presence of yogurt sauce (dairy protein further lowers GI); (3) mixed filling with vegetables. GL per serving calculated as (47 × 19.8 × 2.80) / 100 = 26.0 g. This is a medium-confidence analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~1176 mg per wrap serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-salt preparation, as one wrap provides approximately 49–51% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~26 per serving) from flatbread carbohydrates, partially offset by high protein and low GI (~47)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume a chicken kofta wrap in moderation; the high protein content (~38 g per wrap) and low-to-moderate GI (~47) help attenuate postprandial glucose excursions, but the total carbohydrate load (~55 g per wrap) requires careful portion control and blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~1.9 g/100g; ~5.3 g per wrap) and sodium (~1176 mg per wrap)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the chicken kofta wrap is a relatively lean protein option compared to red-meat alternatives, but the sodium content and moderate saturated fat contribution warrant portion control and preference for grilled over fried preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Kofta Wrap (Turkish: Tavuk Köfte Dürüm) is a Turkish street-food and restaurant dish consisting of grilled spiced minced chicken kofta served inside a thin flatbread (lavash or yufka), accompanied by fresh tomato, lettuce, onion, and a garlic yogurt or white sauce. The kofta is seasoned with cumin, paprika, black pepper, garlic, and parsley, then shaped into elongated patties or cylinders and grilled over charcoal or a flat griddle. The flatbread is briefly warmed on the grill before wrapping. The result is a compact, portable roll with a smoky, savoury flavour, tender juicy chicken interior, and crisp fresh vegetable contrast. Nutritionally, the wrap provides approximately 195 kcal per 100 g, with a strong protein contribution (~13.5 g/100g) from the chicken, moderate carbohydrate (~19.8 g/100g) from the flatbread, and relatively low saturated fat (~1.9 g/100g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 47 (low), making it a more glycaemically favourable option than many other flatbread-based meals. Sodium content is moderate-to-high (~420 mg/100g), primarily from seasoning and sauce. The dish is halal-certified in most Turkish and Middle Eastern restaurants in Singapore. It is commonly served as a lunch or dinner main course and is popular in Turkish and Lebanese cuisine restaurants across Singapore, including Sultan Palace at Boat Quay. Clinically, it is a reasonable protein-rich meal for individuals managing weight or blood glucose, though sodium and total carbohydrate load should be monitored for hypertension and Type 2 Diabetes management.
common_user_queries: What is a chicken kofta wrap?
How many calories in a chicken kofta wrap?

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central for chicken kofta wrap as a composite dish. Nutrient values estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FDC Foundation Foods data for grilled ground chicken, lavash flatbread, yogurt sauce, and fresh vegetables. GI estimated analogously from University of Sydney GI Database pita bread value with protein-adjustment rationale. Confidence is medium due to component-based estimation rather than direct measurement.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data permitted. Nutrient values are per-100g estimates for a typical chicken kofta wrap as served in Turkish restaurants in Singapore (Sultan Palace, Boat Quay). Serving size of 280 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant portion weight for a single wrap. GI of 47 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for chicken kofta wrap was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. GL per serving = (47 × 19.8 × 2.80) / 100 ≈ 26.0. Sodium estimate includes contribution from seasoning, sauce, and flatbread. The food is served by 2 restaurants on field survey data (Sultan Palace - Boat Quay Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine). Price SGD 17.90.
